goal: Add a side-effect-free runtime dry-run route contract that validates a future HTTP request shape and maps it to the BPK-013 dry-run adapter plan without registering a server route or calling the orchestrator. Do not change server.ts, add routes, execute runtime, call providers, write to DB/GitHub, change packages, deploy, or touch frontend files.
scope_out: Do not register a server route or call orchestrateTask.
Do not add provider, DB, GitHub, deploy, package, environment, workflow, or frontend changes.
claims: A future dry-run route request can be validated without adding a route.
Only POST-like requests with explicit confirmation may produce a dry-run route response.
Blocked BPK-013 plans return non-2xx route contracts.
The route contract is side-effect-free and tested independently.
assumptions: Actual server route wiring belongs to a later block.
BPK-013 remains the source of dry-run invocation planning.
The confirmation phrase is a route-contract guard, not an authorization replacement.
dod: builder/src/runtimeDryRunRouteContract.ts exports side-effect-free route contract types and handler function.
The handler validates method, confirm phrase, instruction, and BPK-013 plan status.
The handler returns deterministic HTTP-like status codes and bodies.
builder/tests/runtimeDryRunRouteContract.test.ts covers allow, wrong method, missing confirm, blocked plan, review plan, and no-mutation behavior.
review-packets/BPK-017.md records scope, safety invariants, gates, and verification.
The focused route contract test and npm run typecheck in builder/ pass.
stop_conditions: A real route, orchestrator call, provider call, DB write, GitHub write, package change, deployment change, workflow change, or frontend file becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: BPK-013 created a dry-run adapter plan without runtime wiring. BPK-017 should define the future route contract without changing server routes.
